sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a 5-day history of right knee pain, which started after a skiing accident over the weekend.
He reported twisting his knee when landing from a jump, resulting in pain and swelling.
The patient denies hearing any popping sensation at the time of injury.
He was able to get out of the water with assistance due to the pain and swelling.
The patient has a history of knee pain, but this is the first time he has injured this knee.
He is a frequent water skier and has a boat on Lake Martin.
He denies any numbness or tingling in his foot.
The patient has been taking aleve twice a day and using a cold pack for pain relief, which has provided some relief.
Past medical history: The patient had his tonsils removed.
He has been taking lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension, which he reports has been effective with no side effects.
His blood pressure has been around 125/80mmHg.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure was 120/77mmHg in the office.
Physical exam: On examination, the patient had ecchymosis and edema on the right knee, with an effusion present.
There was pain to palpation of the right medial knee.
The patient had decreased range of motion, a negative varus and valgus test, a negative lachman sign, and palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses.
There was no lower extremity edema.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee, with a possible effusion and inflammation.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily to help reduce swelling and pain.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent future injuries.
I will continue the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
I also ordered a lipid panel to check for any lipid abnormalities.
The patient is due for a tetanus shot, which will be administered.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with the office after receiving the results of the x-ray and lipid panel, and to discuss further management of his knee injury.
